metadata.description: The name of the Logic App / Microsoft Sentinel playbook resource.
Azure region where the playbook will be deployed.
Compatibility parameter from the identity-disruption template. Not used by this device-isolation playbook.
Compatibility parameter from the identity-disruption template. Not used by this device-isolation playbook.
Primary SOC email recipient for production notifications.
Comma-separated CC recipients for production notifications.
Recipient used for test/simulation or disabled-email-control notifications.
Tenant ID used by the Graph alert-detail lookup when using app credentials.
Client/application ID used by the Graph alert-detail lookup.
Key Vault secret name containing the Graph app client secret.
Defender for Endpoint machine ID used when isolation is disabled or simulation routes to a test device.
Windows time zone ID used for after-hours isolation logic.
Start hour for business-hours logic, in 24-hour local time.
End hour for business-hours logic, in 24-hour local time.
Terminate if the Sentinel incident is already closed.
Enable Defender for Endpoint device isolation.
Send production notification emails when true; route to test recipients when false.
Terminate when the incident title indicates the threat was blocked or prevented.
Allow server isolation only during after-hours window when enabled.
Email simulation selector. 0 disables simulation; 1-4 force specific email branches.

body.Subject: DEVICE ISOLATION FAILED - Action Required - AutoContain
body.Body:
AutoContain attempted to isolate the device but was unable to confirm success.
Microsoft Security Controls have determined the following device is at-risk or already compromised.


Analyst Actions Needed:
Review the referenced Incident / Alert
Complete remediation
Notify relevant NSC Group of recovery requirements
Update/Close Incident in Defender


Device Name:
@{variables('DeviceName')}

Reason:
@{outputs('Compose_Incident_URL')}


Trigger:
@{outputs('Summary_Agent')?['lastAssistantMessage']}

inputs.name: BODY
inputs.value: This is a message from Auto-Isolation. Isolation is currently disabled, the test machine machine has been isolated as a test. fix that in the Logic App Parameters. Thank you.

inputs.name: BODY
inputs.value:
Microsoft Security Controls have determined the following Server is at-risk or already compromised.

Analyst Actions Needed:
Review the referenced Incident / Alert
Complete remediation
Notify relevant NSC Group (if needed)
Update/Close Incident in Defender

inputs.name: SUBJECT
inputs.value: Server Has Been Isolated by AutoContain

inputs.name: BODY
inputs.value:
SERVER ISOLATED
Microsoft Security Controls have determined the following Server is at-risk or already compromised.

Analyst Actions Needed:
Review the referenced Incident / Alert
Complete remediation
Notify relevant NSC Group of recovery requirements
Update/Close Incident in Defender

inputs.name: SUBJECT
inputs.value: Workstation Has Been Isolated by AutoContain

inputs.name: BODY
inputs.value:
WORKSTATION ISOLATED
Microsoft Security Controls have determined the following device is at-risk or already compromised.

Analyst Actions Needed:
Review the referenced Incident / Alert
Complete remediation
Notify relevant NSC Group of recovery requirements
Update/Close Incident in Defender

messages.content:
A device, or multiple devices have just been isolated. 

You have been given the list of devices that were isolated and a list of triggered alerts that caused the isolation of that device. Note, that because this processes has taken place, it means that it was determined that automatic remediation missed or did not "prevent" or "block" a process. So please summarize from the point of view of before isolation. Don't even mention the isolation that the current process took. You are writing a summary of why you isolated, so the fact that you took action or isolated is irrelevant. No bold or formatting allowed. You need to write a summary of that alert in only 1-2 lines. Do not return any conversational details, only return the 1-2 line summary. Please also include before the 1-2 line summary the name of the device for example:

DEVICENAME.contoso.com: XXXX 

### Device Names:
@{union(variables('ActiveEntities'), variables('ActiveEntities'))}

### Alerts:
@{outputs('Compose_Alerts')}
